summary.intent: Address two second-pass Codex review findings (T5, T6) on a QR code component implementation: fix an internal spec contradiction about accessibility labeling and fix a potential integer overflow in pixel size calculation.
summary.outcome: Both findings were resolved: the leptos and dioxus adapter specs were fully synced to describe pattern-owned labeling (T5), and the `pixel_size` calculation was refactored to cast to `f64` before arithmetic to prevent `usize` overflow (T6). Tests were added, `xci-fast` passed, and the round-2 fixes were committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a `xci-fast` gate as the pre-push CI check; it must pass before committing and pushing.
Spec files for leptos and dioxus adapters are maintained in parallel under `spec/leptos-components/` and `spec/dioxus-components/` and must be kept in sync manually.
Codex is used as an automated multi-pass code reviewer; findings are addressed in rounds, with replies/resolutions posted per thread before re-triggering.
The QR code component uses a `quiet_zone` field of type `usize`; sizing arithmetic must be done in `f64` to avoid overflow with large values.

code.finding: `pixel_size` calculation previously multiplied `usize` values (module count + `quiet_zone * 2`) before casting to `f64`, risking overflow in debug (panic) and release (wrap). Fixed by casting components to `f64` before arithmetic.
Proptest oracle for QR code sizing was updated to mirror the `f64`-first arithmetic, and the `quiet_zone` strategy was broadened to include `usize::MAX` to exercise the overflow boundary.

Consumer-guarantee, accessibility-notes, and test-oracle sections still instructed implementers to assert root role/label after the T2 sync — creating an internal contradiction. Fixed to describe root as a neutral container with pattern-owned labeling.
Same three stale root-labeling sections as the leptos spec; required identical fixes applied separately since `replace_all` only operates within a single file.

workflow: Codex review runs in multiple passes; unresolved threads from a second pass must be fetched explicitly after polling for the verdict.
When fixing parallel spec files (leptos/dioxus), edits must be applied to each file independently even when the content is identical.
Background tasks (`xci-fast`, Codex polling) are scheduled with `ScheduleWakeup` and produce `task-notification` events when complete.
The commit-push-reply-resolve-retrigger sequence is the established workflow for closing Codex review threads.

friction: T5 spec sync from a prior round (T2) was incomplete — three sections in each adapter spec were missed, requiring a second pass to fully resolve the contradiction.
`replace_all` cannot operate across multiple files, requiring redundant sequential edits for parallel spec files with identical content.
open_items: Codex `@codex review` re-trigger is pending after the round-2 push — a potential third-pass review may surface additional findings.
No mechanism exists to automatically verify that parallel spec files (leptos/dioxus) remain in sync; a future linting or diffing step could prevent incomplete syncs like T5.
